name: Fitts's Law: Target Size and Proximity
trigger: An interactive control (button, link, icon, checkbox, toggle) is being designed or reviewed, especially primary or frequently used actions, small icon buttons, or controls on touch surfaces.
recommendation: Size and place targets so they are fast to acquire: make pointer targets at least 24x24 CSS px (WCAG 2.5.8 minimum) and prefer 44x44 px for primary or touch targets; give frequent actions larger or closer placement; exploit screen edges and corners (infinite-width edges) for high-traffic controls; and keep adequate spacing so adjacent targets are not mis-hit.
rejection: Reject sub-24px touch targets, tightly packed icon clusters with no spacing, primary CTAs given the same small footprint as minor controls, and destructive actions placed adjacent to common ones where a slip causes damage.
validation: Audit interactive elements for a minimum 24x24 px hit area (44x44 px on touch)
Check spacing between adjacent targets meets at least the minimum to avoid accidental activation
Confirm the most frequent action is the largest or most easily reachable
evidence: Fitts, P.M. (1954) movement-time model: acquisition time grows with distance and shrinks with target size
WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) sets a 24x24 CSS px floor
Apple HIG recommends 44x44 pt and Material recommends 48x48 dp touch targets
source: Fitts's Law (1954); WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8; Apple HIG; Material Design
